title: feat: policy compliance history endpoint
summary: Adds a new `GET /v1/policies/{policy_id}/compliance_history` endpoint that returns the full rule compliance evaluation history as an infinitely paginated resource list. A reusable `list_compliance_history` repository method supports list-based filters (`policy_ids`, `assignment_ids`, `model_ids`, `rule_ids`) for use across future endpoints. A new `POLICY_LIST_COMPLIANCE_HISTORY` permission is added to the SpiceDB schema, permissions library, and default org_reader/org_admin roles, and a `created_at` timestamp field is added to `ComplianceRow`.
userImpact: Users can now query the full compliance evaluation history for a policy via the new compliance history endpoint, with support for filtering and infinite pagination.
author: videetparekh1995

title: feat: add workspace compliance overview endpoint (UP-4155)
summary: Adds a new `GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/compliance` endpoint that provides a flat, filterable compliance overview table showing per-rule, per-model compliance status across all policies in a workspace. Introduces a normalized `compliance_rule_statuses` table (serving as a historical audit trail) with an Alembic migration and backfill from existing JSON data, plus dual-write logic to keep both representations in sync. Supports filtering by status, policy, model, rule type, rule ID, frequency, and text search, along with sorting, pagination, and summary rollup counts. A new `WORKSPACE_LIST_POLICY_COMPLIANCE` permission and SpiceDB schema update control access.
userImpact: Users can now query a single workspace-level compliance endpoint to view, filter, sort, and paginate the compliance status of every rule across all policies and models, with summary counts and alert/attestation metadata included in the response.
author: videetparekh1995

title: create audit logging middleware
summary: Adds a new audit logging middleware to the application plane that records API request details including the path called, the authenticated user, path parameters (resource IDs), and response IDs. The middleware is configurable via environment variables and settings, with the ability to disable audit logging entirely. Unit tests and error handling are included.
userImpact: API calls are now audit-logged with details about who made the request, which endpoint was called, and which resources were accessed or returned, improving traceability and security observability.
author: talerez

title: Add package management info
summary: Added package management information to the CLAUDE.md documentation, covering usage of the `uv` package manager. This provides developers with guidance on how packages are managed in the project.
userImpact: Developers now have documented instructions for package management using `uv` in the project's CLAUDE.md file.
author: nori2

title: Enforce 3-day package minimum for uv dependency resolution
summary: Adds a 3-day minimum package age requirement for uv dependency resolution in both the app_plane and arthur-client pyproject.toml configurations. This ensures that only packages published for at least 3 days are considered during dependency resolution, reducing the risk of pulling in newly published or potentially malicious packages.
userImpact: Dependency resolution now enforces a 3-day minimum package age, improving supply-chain security by avoiding freshly published packages.
author: nori2

title: create audit logging tdd
summary: Adds a new technical design document (TDD) for audit logging functionality. The document outlines the design for capturing audit log events from API requests, including resource ID extraction from path parameters, user identification, and a versioned audit log event schema. The resource ID extraction approach was updated during review to dynamically collect all path parameters into a list rather than using a hardcoded single value.
userImpact: No direct user impact.

A minor cleanup removes an unused `type: ignore` comment in the platform bootstrap module.
userImpact: Users can now run multiple stacks simultaneously on the same host without port collisions by setting `SCOPE_APP_PORT`, parameterized Docker Compose ports, and `CORS_EXTRA_ORIGINS` environment variables.

title: Fix alert_interval migration ORM breakage
summary: Fixed the `alert_interval` Alembic migration (`0d2efdd3920a`) which imported the `DBAlertRule` ORM model to query and update rows mid-migration. As the ORM model evolved with new columns (e.g. `policy_alert_rule_id`), SQLAlchemy would generate SELECTs referencing columns that don't yet exist at that point in the migration chain, breaking fresh database bootstraps with `UndefinedColumn` errors. The fix replaces all ORM queries with raw SQL `UPDATE` statements decoupled from the evolving model. A Docker Compose smoke test CI job was also added to catch migration failures like this in the future.
userImpact: Fresh database bootstraps (`alembic upgrade head`) now complete successfully without `UndefinedColumn` errors caused by the alert_interval migration.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Bump upsolve to 0.4.24
summary: Bumps the upsolve container image from version 0.4.23 to 0.4.24 and replaces the database patch file with an updated version. This is a routine dependency version bump.

Changes are limited to pyproject.toml and uv.lock in the app_plane scope.

title: Add scheduled compliance job bootstrapping
summary: Introduces the `SCHEDULE_COMPLIANCE_JOBS` job kind with full API spec, DB polymorphic type, and repository registration (infinite retries, 45MB, MEDIUM priority). When a policy assignment is created for a model, a per-model compliance schedule chain is automatically bootstrapped using a deterministic `uuid5` schedule_id, skipping creation if a chain already exists. The schedule is cancelled when the last policy assignment is removed from a model, with a safeguard that stops job re-enqueuing if no assignments remain. Also includes a bootstrap migration for existing assignments, a design document, and a CI lint path fix for uv migration.
userImpact: When a compliance policy is assigned to a model, a recurring compliance job schedule is now automatically created and managed per model, eliminating the need for manual scheduling setup and ensuring compliance jobs run continuously as long as at least one policy assignment exists.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Clean up the data_plane code that's been migrated to Github
summary: Removes the entire `scope/data_plane` codebase (100 files) from this repository, as it has been migrated to a separate GitHub repository. The deleted code includes connectors (BigQuery, S3, GCS, ODBC, Shield), job executors, aggregators, health checks, configuration, and utility modules.
userImpact: No direct user impact — the data_plane functionality continues to live in its new GitHub repository.
author: nori2

title: UP-4128 return entire compliance job instead of just ID
summary: The policy_assignment API resource now returns the full compliance job object instead of only the compliance job ID. This includes detailed job metadata such as state, timestamps, trigger type, job spec, and triggered-by user information, reducing the need for follow-up API calls to retrieve job details.
userImpact: Users querying policy assignments now receive the complete compliance job details inline, eliminating the need for a separate API call to look up job status and metadata.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Submit compliance jobs when policy assignments are created
summary: Compliance check jobs are now automatically triggered when policy assignments are created, when alert or attestation rules are created/updated/deleted, and when attestations are submitted. The compliance check logic was moved from routers into operators to ensure jobs are triggered consistently regardless of call site. A `raise_on_empty` parameter was added to `check_policy_compliance` so that rule CRUD on policies with no assignments is a silent no-op, while the explicit check endpoint still raises an error. API mutations and compliance job submissions are now committed atomically in a single transaction.
userImpact: Users now receive an immediate `compliance_job_id` when creating policy assignments, and compliance checks are automatically triggered on rule changes and attestation submissions — eliminating the need to manually initiate compliance checks after these operations.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4154 Fire webhook on compliance status transition to NON_COMPLIANT
summary: When a policy assignment transitions to NON_COMPLIANT and the enforcement delay has expired, the system now automatically fires the policy's configured notification webhook. A new `last_violation_notified_at` field is tracked on assignments and exposed on the `PolicyAssignment` and `PolicyAssignmentDetail` API models. A new `execute_webhook_with_context` method was added to `WebhookExecutor` to support compliance-specific template rendering, filtering context to only non-compliant rules.
userImpact: Users with configured policy webhooks now receive automatic notifications when an assignment becomes non-compliant (after any enforcement delay), and can see the `last_violation_notified_at` timestamp on policy assignment API responses.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 compliance check endpoints
summary: Adds three new compliance check endpoints: `POST /api/v1/policies/:policy_id/check_compliance`, `POST /api/v1/models/:model_id/check_compliance`, and `POST /api/v1/policy_assignments/:assignment_id/check_compliance`. Each endpoint triggers compliance check jobs for the relevant policy assignments and returns the list of created/queued jobs. The `compliance_job_id` is set on assignments when a check is initiated and cleared when the compliance status is subsequently set.
userImpact: Users can now trigger on-demand compliance checks at the policy, model, or individual assignment level via dedicated API endpoints and track the resulting jobs.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated Renovate Bot update of two Python dependencies: boto3 from 1.42.77 to 1.42.78 and pydantic_core from 2.42.0 to 2.44.0. The boto3 patch includes API changes for bedrock-agentcore, neptunedata, and omics services.

Adds `created_at` filters to model list alerts APIs, fixes crashing endpoints, corrects the `policy_assignment_delete` permission to target the correct resource, and grants additional permissions to the data plane role needed for compliance job execution. Includes a new database migration adding a compliance status detail column.
userImpact: Users now receive a richer compliance status response showing per-rule compliant/non-compliant breakdowns with alert and attestation details, and can filter model alerts by `created_at`; previously crashing endpoints and permission errors are also resolved.

userImpact: No direct user impact.

title: Policy assignment endpoints with materialization and authz
summary: Implements full CRUD API endpoints for policy assignments, attestations, and compliance status with SpiceDB-based authorization. Policy assignment creation materializes alert rules on each target model and enforces dual permission checks (on the policy and on each target model). The architecture splits the monolithic policies operator/repository into per-resource pairs (PolicyAssignmentsOperator/Repository, AttestationsOperator/Repository), with cross-resource logic like alert rule materialization and cascade deletion handled at the operator level. Adds input validation for group/webhook existence and alert rule SQL queries on policy create/update, duplicate assignment detection (409), model existence validation (404), and a comprehensive end-to-end lifecycle test. Also cherry-picks compliance job improvements (detailed compliance status model with per-rule results, created_at filters, data plane permissions).
userImpact: Users can now create, list, and delete policy assignments to models (with automatic alert rule materialization), record and query attestations per assignment or model with filtering by latest/valid status, and set detailed compliance status including per-rule results — all protected by fine-grained authorization checks. Policy and attestation responses now return full user objects instead of just user IDs.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add NEEDS_ATTENTION compliance status for enforcement delay period
summary: Introduces a new `NEEDS_ATTENTION` compliance status for policy assignments that have rule violations but are still within the enforcement delay grace period. This status is informational only — no webhooks or tags are triggered — giving model owners time to remediate before the system automatically promotes to `NON_COMPLIANT` once the delay expires. The technical design documentation is updated to reflect the new enum value, evaluation logic, compliance metrics class labels, and job lifecycle changes.
userImpact: Model owners now see a `NEEDS_ATTENTION` compliance status during the enforcement delay grace period, giving them clear, early warning of policy violations and time to remediate before full `NON_COMPLIANT` enforcement actions (webhooks, tags) are triggered.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 api tweaks for job
summary: Multiple API updates to support the compliance check job: added `assignment_id` filtering to model list policy assignments and alert rules APIs, added `latest` and `valid` filters to the model list attestations API, exposed `policy_assignment_id` and `policy_alert_rule_id` fields in alert rule responses, and blocked PATCH/DELETE operations on policy-managed alert rules. Also fixes a SQLAlchemy relationship warning in storage.
userImpact: Users can now filter policy assignments, alert rules, and attestations by assignment ID, latest, and validity status, and policy-managed alert rules are protected from accidental modification or deletion.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-4128 add compliance check job API spec
summary: Adds API spec support for a new "compliance check" job type. This includes a new database migration adding a `policy_assignment_id` column to job specs, updates to the API models, storage layer, job spec extractor, and jobs repository to handle the new compliance check job configuration.
userImpact: Users can now create and configure compliance check jobs via the API, enabling automated compliance workflows tied to policy assignments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: uv for dependency management
summary: Migrated dependency management from pip/requirements.txt (and Poetry for arthur-client) to uv across the app-plane and arthur-client packages. This includes replacing all references to system Python and pip with uv commands in local-dev scripts, CI pipelines, Dockerfiles, and build scripts, as well as introducing pyproject.toml and uv.lock files for pinned, reproducible dependency resolution.
userImpact: Developers now use uv for dependency management, gaining faster installs, pinned lock files for reproducible builds, and a simplified setup workflow across local development, CI, and Docker environments.
author: nori2

title: Implement policy alert and attestation rule CRUD with authz and propagation
summary: Implements full CRUD endpoints for policy alert rules and policy attestation rules, including static SQL validation for alert rules, live propagation of changes to materialized alert rules on assigned models, and SpiceDB authorization enforcement on all new endpoints. The architecture is refactored to split rule logic into dedicated `PolicyRulesRepository` and per-rule-type operators, and the integration test suite is consolidated into a single `test_policy_full_lifecycle` covering the entire policy workflow from creation through rule management, assignments, compliance, and deletion.
userImpact: Users can now create, list, read, update, and delete policy alert rules and attestation rules via the API, with changes to alert rules automatically propagated to all models the policy is assigned to, and proper authorization checks enforced on every operation.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Policy CRUD (Create/Read/Update/Delete) with Inline Rules
summary: Implements full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) for policies with inline alert and attestation rules. Adds a `PoliciesRepository` supporting atomic policy creation with inline rules, eager-loaded retrieval, paginated listing with name filter and sort, partial updates, and cascade deletes. A `PoliciesOperator` wraps repository calls with transaction commits, and five router endpoints (POST, GET list, GET by ID, PATCH, DELETE) are wired to real operator calls with SpiceDB permission checks enforced. Twelve policy endpoints were removed from the authz whitelist now that proper permission checks are in place. Comprehensive API endpoint lifecycle tests cover the full CRUD flow, validation, filtering, sorting, and error cases using the generated client.
userImpact: Users can now create, list, retrieve, update, and delete policies with inline alert and attestation rules through fully functional API endpoints with enforced permission checks.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add an alert rule validation endpoint without modelId requirement
summary: Adds a new alert rule validation endpoint that does not require a modelId parameter. The endpoint is whitelisted from authorization checks (authz) while still requiring authentication (authn), allowing users to validate alert rule configurations without needing to specify a model. Supporting changes include new validation logic, query modifier updates, and corresponding unit tests.
userImpact: Users can now validate alert rule configurations without needing to provide a modelId, simplifying the validation workflow for alert rules that are not yet associated with a specific model.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Add policy ORM models, database relationships, and migration
summary: Introduces new SQLAlchemy ORM models and an Alembic migration for the policy governance system: `policies`, `policy_alert_rules`, `policy_attestation_rules`, `policy_model_assignments`, and `attestation_records` tables. Adds nullable foreign keys to the existing `alert_rules` table to link materialized alert rules back to their policy source, with a check constraint ensuring referential consistency. Also extends the `AuthZRelationship` enum with `POLICY_PARENT` and `MODEL_PARENT` for dual-parent SpiceDB relationships on policy-model assignments, and validates compliance status (`Pending`, `Compliant`, `Non-compliant`, `Needs Attention`) at the API level rather than via a database constraint.
userImpact: Users will be able to create and manage governance policies that attach alert and attestation rules to models, track per-model compliance status, and have materialized alert rules linked back to their policy source.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Use oidc
summary: Migrates CI/CD deployment pipelines from AWS access key-based authentication to OpenID Connect (OIDC) across all deployment environments (aws-dev, aws-prod, dev-sts, expel-sts, inquirer-sts). Adds an account verification step to validate the correct AWS account is targeted. Also removes the axios-sts-platform environment entirely and cleans up related CI configuration.
userImpact: Deployments now authenticate to AWS using OIDC instead of static access keys, improving security posture and eliminating the need to manage long-lived credentials.
author: jdb-arthur

title: [no-ref] set renovate minimumReleaseAge 3 days
summary: Configures Renovate bot with a `minimumReleaseAge` setting to delay package upgrades, giving package maintainers time to identify and revoke potentially malicious versions before they are adopted. The final configuration sets a 14-day minimum release age in `renovate.json`.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Atomic policy creation with inline rules and field renames
summary: The `POST /v1/organization/policies` endpoint now supports atomic policy creation with inline alert rules and attestation rules, requiring at least one rule at creation time (enforced via a model validator returning 422 on violation). The create policy response is simplified to a `PolicySummary` (id, name, description). Additionally, fields are renamed for clarity: `grace_period_days` → `enforcement_delay_days`, `grace_period_ends_at` → `enforcement_starts_at`, and `interval_days` → `validity_period_days` across all design docs and code.
userImpact: Users creating policies must now include at least one alert or attestation rule inline in the request body, and must use the renamed fields (`enforcement_delay_days`, `enforcement_starts_at`, `validity_period_days`); the response shape is now a simplified `PolicySummary` instead of the full `Policy` object.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Redistribute policy permissions across existing roles
summary: Redistributes policy-related permissions across existing roles instead of introducing a premature Policy Manager role. Model-level policy operations (assign/remove policy, attest, check compliance) are moved to the Project Admin role, enforcing the principle that org-level roles grant no model-level access. Adds a `NEEDS_ATTENTION` compliance status for grace-period violations, introduces an `is_assigned_in` reverse relation on policies so model owners can read assigned policies without org-level `policy_read`, and removes dead SpiceDB code that was never used in permission resolution.
userImpact: Project Admins can now assign/remove policies, attest, and check compliance on models they have access to, while model owners can read policies assigned to their models without needing org-level policy read permissions; Project Readers are now strictly read-only with no compliance-check ability.
author: videetparekh1995

userImpact: Database connections are now properly cleaned up on server shutdown, preventing lingering connections that could exhaust database connection limits.

title: fix users being able to view unauthorized models and alerts
summary: Fixed an authorization bypass where users with governance_admin and workspace_reader access (but limited project_reader access) could see applications and alerts belonging to projects they were not authorized to view. The fix enforces project-level access checks in the models repository, alerts repository, and workspace router so that the "all applications" tab and alert endpoints only return data for projects the user has permission to access.
userImpact: Users with mixed workspace and project roles will now only see models and alerts for projects they are explicitly authorized to access, preventing unauthorized visibility into other projects.

summary: Fixed custom aggregation test results to return float values instead of rounding them to integers. The API models and metrics repository were updated to correctly preserve float precision, aligning the API response with how the database stores metric values as floats. Unit tests were updated accordingly.
userImpact: Custom aggregation test results now return accurate float values instead of incorrectly rounded integers, ensuring metric precision is preserved end-to-end.

The `agent_read` permission was moved from the `workspace_read_all` role to `workspace_reader`, and the filtering logic was added at the repository level to only return agent data for models the user is permitted to view. A SpiceDB schema migration removes the old `agent_read` relationships, and new unit tests validate the scoped access behavior.
userImpact: Users now only see registered agent data for models they have been explicitly granted access to, improving data visibility scoping and enforcing model-level permissions on all registered agent list endpoints.

author: jdb-arthur

title: Add policies technical design document
summary: Adds comprehensive technical design documentation for org-level policies, covering database schema (policies, alert rules, attestation rules, model assignments, attestation records), entity relationships, compliance state machine (GRACE_PERIOD, COMPLIANT, NON_COMPLIANT, PENDING_ATTESTATION), apply/unapply timeline flows, API endpoints with request/response schemas, and SpiceDB permissions. The design was iterated extensively through review to refine compliance evaluation (moved to data plane), simplify attestation to sign-off only (no approval workflow), flatten API endpoints, enforce daily alert rule intervals, require policy owners (as groups) with webhooks, and align permissions granularly across sub-resources.
userImpact: No direct user impact — this is a design document laying the groundwork for upcoming org-level policy features that will allow defining alert rules and attestation requirements applied to models/applications across workspaces.

title: remove old model reg when switching agent reg
summary: When switching which agent is associated with a model, the system now properly removes the model ID from the previously registered agent and deletes the old PARENT_MODEL relationship from SpiceDB before creating the new one. Additionally, the `find_agent` lookup was made more strict to prevent overly broad searches (e.g., matching any agent in a workspace).
userImpact: Reassigning a model to a different agent now correctly cleans up the old agent-model relationship, preventing stale registrations from persisting.

title: Upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgrades the Upsolve dependency to a newer version to fix an issue where adding charts to workspace dashboards was not working correctly. The change is a single-line update in the Upsolve Dockerfile.
userImpact: Users can now successfully add charts to workspace dashboards, which was previously broken.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Feat/mute agents
summary: Adds the ability to mute and unmute unregistered agents using a `muted_until` timestamp, allowing users to suppress agents for a specified duration rather than permanently. A new `is_muted` filter is added to the list unregistered agents endpoint, defaulting to showing only non-muted agents. Includes a new database migration adding a `muted_until` column to the agents table, a new permission for updating unregistered agents, and updates to the authorization schema.
userImpact: Users can now mute unregistered agents for a specified duration via a PATCH endpoint and filter the unregistered agents list by mute status, with muted agents hidden by default.
author: talerez

title: upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgrades the upsolve dependency (via Dockerfile version bump) to fix an issue where database migrations were not running correctly on both fresh and existing installs. The fix was verified to work for upgrades from the 0.4.14 release as well as fresh installations.
userImpact: Database migrations now run correctly on both fresh and existing installs, resolving failures that previously occurred during setup or upgrade.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Bug fix: PUT Agents un-sets model id and unregisters registered agents
summary: Fixed a bug where PUT requests to the Agents endpoint would inadvertently un-set the model ID and unregister previously registered agents. The fix includes corrections to the agents repository logic and related database migration (constraint names and migration script), along with updates to the unregistered agents upload script.
userImpact: Updating an agent via PUT no longer accidentally clears the model ID or unregisters the agent, preserving agent registration state as expected.
author: ianmcgraw

title: create put agents route
summary: Adds a new PUT endpoint for creating/adding agents, along with supporting changes to the agent repository, API models, authorization schema (SpiceDB parent model relationship), and permission definitions. A demo script for uploading unregistered agents is also included. The agent repository received significant expansion (+397 lines) to support the new creation flow, including relationship filters for delete and removal of legacy unregistered_agent_id references.
userImpact: Users can now add new agents via the PUT agents API endpoint.
author: talerez

title: add functionality to dummy adg routes
summary: Implements functionality for all ADG (Agent Data Gateway) routes, replacing previously stubbed/dummy endpoints with real logic. Adds a new registered agents loop, includes data plane infrastructure as a relationship on DBAgent (returned in AgentResponse), and updates the Model response object to return an agent_id. A new agents repository is introduced to support these operations.
userImpact: Users can now interact with fully functional ADG agent routes, including listing, retrieving, and managing registered agents, and will see agent_id and infrastructure information in Model and Agent API responses.
author: talerez

title: Revert Upsolve Upgrade
summary: Reverts the Upsolve upgrade from version 0.4.15 back to a previous version. The 0.4.15 release fixed migrations for fresh environments but broke migrations on existing environments that had previously deployed 0.4.14, causing a "type 'actor_type' already exists" error during database bootstrap and preventing db-ops from starting.
userImpact: Existing environments that were failing to start due to broken Upsolve database migrations will now bootstrap successfully again.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Latest upsolve fixes
summary: Applies performance fixes and corrections to workspace and project dashboards via upsolve patch updates. Includes a minor fix to an Alembic migration for LLM and tool aggregations on agents, a Dockerfile update, and a renamed/updated upsolve patch file.
userImpact: Workspace and project dashboards should now load faster and display data more reliably after these upsolve and migration fixes.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Retain unregistered agents and model maps, add new model table
summary: Introduces a new `agents` table alongside the existing `unregistered_agents` table to support a gradual migration path. The Alembic migration creates `agents`, `llm_models`, and `data_sources` tables, adds dual/triple ownership columns (agent_id) to `tools`, `sub_agents`, and `model_specs`, and backfills data from `unregistered_agents` into the new `agents` table for rows with a non-null task_id. All existing `unregistered_agents` relationships and columns are preserved for backward compatibility.
userImpact: Registered agents are now tracked in a dedicated table with richer metadata (creation source, rules, autocreation flag), enabling future agent management capabilities while preserving all existing unregistered agent functionality.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Clean up migrations
summary: Cleans up two existing database migrations: removes an explicit "COMMIT" statement from migration `0a02c3edecce` and eliminates the use of ORM models (db.storage) in migration `e43efdac3d12`, replacing them with raw SQL or direct table operations. These changes improve migration reliability and follow best practices for Alembic migrations.
userImpact: No direct user impact.

title: Feature/register agent

The `PostTaskRequest` now accepts `AgentMetadata` containing GCP project, region, and resource ID, and the job serialization pipeline (`DBCreateTaskJobSpec`, `JobSpecDBTranslator`) was updated to properly persist and deserialize this metadata through the job queue. A corresponding database migration was added to store task metadata on the create-task job.
userImpact: Users can now include agent source/provenance information (GCP project, region, resource ID) when creating tasks, enabling tracking of where registered agents originate.

The deploy script received significant enhancements (+56 lines), and the root HA stack template was substantially refactored (-244/+134 lines) for cleaner organization. Minor updates were also made to the scope alarms template and GitLab CI configuration.
userImpact: Users deploying the metrics database HA stack benefit from better-organized CloudFormation parameter groups, an improved RHEL AMI search command, and a cleaner deployment experience.

title: UP-3501 support csv for bucket connectors
summary: Adds CSV file support for bucket-based connectors (e.g., S3). When creating a bucket-based dataset, users can now specify optional CSV parameters (such as delimiter and encoding) which are used to correctly parse CSV files. A migration adds the new CSV fields to the connector's dataset locator schema, and a sample messy CSV binary classification dataset generator is included for testing.
userImpact: Users can now connect to CSV files stored in cloud buckets (e.g., S3) by configuring optional CSV parsing parameters when creating a bucket-based dataset, enabling inference deep dive and metrics to work seamlessly with CSV data.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Agent Scanning functionality
summary: Introduces agent scanning functionality, including automatic creation of a System Project in every workspace, a new Discover Agents job type, GCP attributes added to the creation source model, and a registerModel capability that creates a new Task for discovered agents. Database migrations add a system project flag, workspace/data-plane lookback fields, and GCP creation source info.
userImpact: Users now have automatic agent discovery within their workspaces, with each workspace receiving a system project and the ability to register discovered agents as new tasks.

summary: Updated the Upsolve database patch and Dockerfile to export a new agent dashboard. The change includes a renamed DB patch file and a minor Dockerfile modification to support the new dashboard configuration.

title: Updates for Improved Agentic Metrics
summary: Bumps the Arthur common library and adds a database migration to update existing agentic dataset schemas and model metrics configurations to use improved aggregations. The migration (targeting existing agentic models) aligns stored schemas and metric configs with the latest agentic metrics definitions from the updated engine.
userImpact: Existing agentic models are automatically migrated to use improved metric aggregations, providing more accurate and up-to-date agentic metrics without manual reconfiguration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-3509: Add custom agg validation
summary: Added validation logic for custom aggregations in the API models layer. The change includes new validation rules in the custom aggregations model and corresponding unit tests to verify the validation behavior.
userImpact: Users will now receive clearer validation errors when submitting invalid custom aggregation configurations, preventing misconfigured aggregations from being created.
author: alex380125

title: UP-3277: Add OBDC connector into local integration tests
summary: Added a local ODBC connector (PostgreSQL-based) to the integration test environment via Docker Compose, enabling fully offline integration test runs without depending on an external ODBC connector. This includes a new SQL initialization script with test data, updated test cases, revised settings, and updated README documentation with instructions for running the tests locally.
userImpact: No direct user impact — this change improves the developer experience by allowing ODBC-related integration tests to run fully offline in a local Docker environment.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: Add Eval data to task cache requests in the API
summary: Adds state storage and API support for a Task's Evals, Continuous Evals, and Transforms. This includes a new database migration to persist eval-related data, updated API models and repository layers to handle the new fields, and extended task cache/sync responses to return eval information. Filtering by model problem type and data plane infrastructure is also included.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve Eval, Continuous Eval, and Transform data as part of task cache API requests, enabling richer task state visibility without additional API calls.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Agent Governance Demo Overview Page APIs
summary: Adds a suite of new APIs to support the Agent Governance Demo Overview Page. This includes full CRUDL operations for unregistered agents, new workspace-level endpoints for alerts and models, tools and sub-agent management APIs for both models and unregistered agents, and new query filters for engine infrastructure and model problem type. Model API responses are also updated to return the entire data plane object instead of just the `data_plane_infrastructure` field.
userImpact: Users can now manage unregistered agents (create, read, update, delete, list), retrieve workspace-level alerts and models, manage tools and sub-agents on models and unregistered agents, and filter models by engine infrastructure and problem type.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [No-ref] Split helm for stateful and stateless services
summary: Splits the existing Helm chart into separate charts for stateful and stateless services. Stateful service templates (PostgreSQL, SpiceDB, TimescaleDB) have been moved into a new `stateful_services` Helm chart with its own values, secrets example, and documentation, while the `control_plane` chart has been slimmed down to focus on stateless services.
userImpact: Operators deploying via Helm now manage stateful services (PostgreSQL, SpiceDB, TimescaleDB) through a dedicated `stateful_services` chart, enabling independent lifecycle management and upgrades for stateful and stateless components.
author: milosz16

title: Set the ALB idle timeout to 3600 seconds to handle long requests uploading large metrics data set
summary: Increases the Application Load Balancer (ALB) idle timeout from the default to 3600 seconds (1 hour) in the CloudFormation stack configuration. This prevents premature connection termination when users upload large metrics data sets that require long-running requests.
userImpact: Users uploading large metrics data sets will no longer experience request timeouts caused by the ALB closing idle connections prematurely.

title: Full arthur engine installs
summary: Adds full Arthur Engine CPU install configurations for Expel and Prod environments, refactors CI pipeline logic per environment, and introduces GitLab pipeline failure notifications routed to PagerDuty. Axios is intentionally skipped as it is being deprecated. Also includes reusable STS role assumption scripts, environment configuration tests, and data plane stack management updates.

Includes comprehensive unit tests for the new role configuration and permission inheritance.
userImpact: Users assigned the governance admin or workspace admin role can now view workspace governance resources via the new WORKSPACE_GOVERNANCE_VIEW permission.

summary: Fixed a bug where users in IDP-controlled groups could not assign role bindings. The operator now uses SpiceDB and the token context to resolve group memberships, correctly accounting for IDP-managed users.
userImpact: Users who belong to IDP-managed groups can now correctly assign role bindings to other groups, resolving a permission error that previously blocked this action.

title: [no-ref] fix keycloak local dev https required error
summary: Fixes a Keycloak SSL-required error that broke local development after recent Docker updates. Newer Docker versions route port-forwarded requests from IPs that Keycloak no longer recognizes as local, causing HTTPS enforcement. The fix updates the Keycloak bootstrap configuration and docker-compose setup to set `ssl-required` to `none` for local development.
userImpact: Local developers using newer versions of Docker Desktop can now successfully run and sign into the application without encountering Keycloak HTTPS-required errors.

summary: Updated the `ArthurOIDCMetadata` discovery class to accept a `verify_ssl` parameter, allowing clients to control SSL certificate verification when making secure requests. The change propagates this parameter through the device authorizer and OIDC discovery modules.
userImpact: Users can now bypass or configure SSL certificate verification when connecting the Arthur client to environments with custom or self-signed certificates.

title: UP-2842: Add support for custom metrics in aggregation matcher
summary: Adds support for custom metrics in the aggregation matcher, enabling custom aggregations to be matched alongside default metrics. This involves changes to the custom aggregations repository, models operator, datasets repository, and projects router, along with unified aggregation loading logic. Extensive test coverage was added and updated to validate the new matching behavior, including RBAC and workspace-scoped scenarios.
userImpact: Users can now define custom metrics that are recognized and matched by the aggregation matcher, allowing custom aggregations to participate in the same workflows as built-in default metrics.

summary: Adds a new CI/CD pipeline job and deployment scripts to deploy the Arthur Engine on AWS dev infrastructure using CloudFormation templates. The changes include downloading Arthur Engine CFT files, configuring GPU workers, managing stack naming/namespaces, setting dynamic engine versioning, and adding success tags and cleanup for old data plane stacks.
userImpact: Arthur Engine is now deployed to the AWS dev environment, enabling inference engine capabilities for internal development and testing.

title: Avoid cert copy that's not working in JPMC
summary: Removes a certificate copy step in the upsolve Docker setup that was failing in the JPMC environment. The Dockerfile and docker-entrypoint script were modified to bypass the non-functional cert copy operation.
userImpact: Resolves a certificate copy failure that was preventing the upsolve service from working correctly in the JPMC environment.

title: Postgres HA with streaming replication and auto-failover with Patroni and etcd
summary: Adds a RHEL-compatible Docker Compose setup for a 3-node Postgres high-availability cluster using Patroni for orchestration and etcd for Raft-based consensus coordination. The cluster consists of 1 primary and 2 streaming replicas with automatic primary failover. Includes environment configs for each node, a custom Patroni Dockerfile, entrypoint script, and Patroni YAML configuration template.
userImpact: Users can now deploy a production-ready, highly available 3-node Postgres cluster with streaming replication and automatic failover using the provided Docker Compose configuration.

title: add validations and unit tests for custom aggregations
summary: Centralized all custom aggregation validations into a single `validate_custom_aggregation_spec_schema` function, replacing scattered model-level validators. This ensures the validation endpoint and the creation endpoint both return a comprehensive list of all validation passes and failures at once, rather than surfacing errors one at a time. Extensive unit tests were added covering nested columns, aliases, SQL validations, and timestamp column type checks.
userImpact: Users configuring custom aggregation schemas now receive a comprehensive list of all validation errors and successes in a single response, rather than having to fix and resubmit one error at a time.

userImpact: Users can now configure the Arthur client to skip SSL certificate verification for OAuth sessions, enabling connectivity in environments with self-signed or untrusted certificates.

title: 4/13/2026 Release
summary: This release includes multiple feature additions and improvements: unified evaluators with continuous evaluation UX in the Evaluate section, guardrails and retry logic for text-to-SQL analytics agent tools, tag filtering for Platform Management Page, scheduled compliance job executor, and a new policy alert rule check count metric. The release also migrates the project from Poetry to uv for dependency management, adds configurable port and CORS support for multi-stack development, and includes various dependency updates and bug fixes.
userImpact: Users can now use unified evaluators with improved continuous evaluation workflows, enjoy better text-to-SQL reliability with guardrails, filter by tags on the Platform Management Page, and benefit from scheduled compliance job execution and enhanced development flexibility.
author: ntatsumi

title: Have the SDK set GenAI Engine task ID to OTLP spans
summary: The SDK now includes the GenAI Engine task_id as an attribute in OTLP spans when initialized. Previously, Arthur.__init__ accepted task_id but only used it for prompt fetch API calls, leaving it out of OpenTelemetry resource attributes. This change ensures task_id is consistently propagated to observability backends.
userImpact: Users can now see GenAI Engine task IDs in their OTLP span attributes, enabling better correlation and filtering of traces in observability platforms.
author: ntatsumi

title: feat: add policy_alert_rule_check_count metric
summary: Adds a new `policy_alert_rule_check_count` metric to the compliance policy check executor that tracks rule violations with dimensions for policy, assignment, model, rule, and compliance status. This enables historic line graphs of rule violations over time, segmented by individual rules across policies, providing better observability into compliance trends.
userImpact: Users can now visualize historic trends of individual rule violations across policies using the new `policy_alert_rule_check_count` metric, enabling better monitoring and analysis of compliance patterns over time.
author: videetparekh

title: [no-ref] handle unknown model cost gracefully
summary: Previously, API calls would fail with an exception when using models that LiteLLM doesn't have pricing data for. The fix now gracefully handles unknown model costs by defaulting to $0.00, allowing the completion to succeed instead of raising an error during cost calculation.
userImpact: Users can now use unknown or newer model variants without the API call failing due to missing cost information.
author: mcgrawia

title: UP-4109 eval bulk testing
summary: Adds bulk testing functionality to run continuous evaluations over multiple trace IDs at once, with a new test run dialog, history view, and API endpoints for managing test runs. Also improves the traces view by adding a loading state to prevent showing "no traces" message during initial data load.
userImpact: Users can now execute a continuous evaluation against a set of trace IDs in bulk and track test run progress and history from a dedicated UI interface.
author: mcgrawia

title: feat(ui): unified evaluators + continuous evals UX in Evaluate section
summary: Redesigned the Evaluate section UI to unify evaluator and continuous eval management into a single integrated workflow. The 3-tab layout (Evals Management, Continuous Evals, Results) is now a 2-tab design (Evaluators and Results) with expandable accordion rows for each evaluator that reveal all continuous evals running off it. Continuous eval creation, editing, and deletion can now happen inline within the evaluator row, and staleness warnings alert users when a continuous eval is pinned to an outdated evaluator version.
userImpact: Users can now manage evaluators and their associated continuous evals in a single unified view, avoiding context-switching between tabs, with clearer visibility into version dependencies and inline controls for continuous eval lifecycle management.

title: address CCR chatbot comments

userImpact: Chatbot functionality is improved with better error handling, more robust API interactions, and enhanced tool function support.

summary: Added environment variable support for configurable server ports and CORS origins in genai-engine, enabling multiple stacks to run simultaneously without port conflicts. The `GENAI_ENGINE_PORT` env var controls server startup (default 3030), `CORS_EXTRA_ORIGINS` extends allowed CORS origins via comma-separated list, and docker-compose.yml now parameterizes host-mapped ports for both database and engine components.
userImpact: Users can now run multiple genai-engine stacks on the same machine by setting environment variables (GENAI_ENGINE_PORT, GENAI_DB_PORT, CORS_EXTRA_ORIGINS) to customize ports and CORS policies, eliminating port collision issues during multi-stack development.

title: feat(analytics-agent): Add guardrails and retry loop for textToSQL tool
summary: Added Arthur Engine hallucination guardrails to the analytics agent's SQL execution tool, which validates natural language summaries of query results against raw data. When Arthur's validation API detects a hallucination, the agent automatically retries self-correction up to 4 times with feedback on the blocking reason. Each attempt is tracked as a child GUARDRAIL span in the observability trace view.
userImpact: Users can now enable guardrail validation via ARTHUR_GUARDRAILS_ENABLED to detect and automatically correct SQL result summaries that hallucinate or distort query data, improving accuracy and trustworthiness of agent responses.

title: Release Arthur engine 4/2/26
summary: Release of Arthur engine version 4/2/26, a routine release containing dependency updates, bug fixes, and incremental feature improvements. This release includes fixes for special character encoding in entity names, Claude Code tracer reliability improvements, and UI enhancements for evaluator and prompt version handling.
userImpact: No direct user impact - this is a maintenance release with internal improvements and dependency updates.
author: mcgrawia

title: Add scheduled compliance job executor
summary: Adds `SCHEDULE_COMPLIANCE_JOBS` executor that automatically generates recurring compliance policy check jobs on a 24-hour interval per model. The executor follows the self-chaining pattern established by the metrics scheduler, creating k=3 compliance jobs plus one self-referential chain job per execution. On-demand compliance checks triggered manually remain unaffected and fully independent.
userImpact: Models with assigned compliance policies now automatically receive periodic policy compliance checks every 24 hours, eliminating the need for manual on-demand compliance testing while maintaining full control over ad-hoc compliance verification.
author: videetparekh

title: UP-3859: Tag filtering for Platform Management Page
summary: Added tag filtering to the Platform Management Page, allowing users to view and filter prompts by production and custom tags. Tags are now displayed on each prompt in the PromptsTable, with multi-select filter controls that support server-side filtering for accurate pagination and tag collection across all prompts.
userImpact: Users can now see which tags are applied to each prompt and filter the prompts table by selecting one or more tags to view only relevant prompts.
author: iabouhashish

title: UP-3149-trace-retention
summary: Implemented trace retention policy management that allows users to configure automatic deletion of traces and spans based on retention age. The system includes a background service that periodically identifies and deletes expired traces in batches, with multi-node concurrency safety via SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Database indices on trace.end_time optimize retention queries, and a circuit breaker pattern protects against cascading failures.
userImpact: Users can now set organization-wide trace retention policies through the Settings modal, automatically removing old traces and spans to manage storage costs and comply with data retention requirements.
author: martin-arthur-ai

title: RFC 7230 - a server MUST NOT send Transfer-Encoding in 1xx or 204 response
summary: The server now correctly omits the Transfer-Encoding header from 1xx (informational) and 204 No Content responses, as required by RFC 7230 §3.3.1 and RFC 9112 §6.1. Previously, the server could include this header in responses where it must not appear, violating the HTTP specification.
userImpact: HTTP responses with status codes 1xx and 204 are now RFC-compliant and will not incorrectly include the Transfer-Encoding header.
author: ntatsumi

title: Make a feature flag for transfer encoding middleware
summary: Added a feature flag to control the transfer encoding middleware in the GenAI engine. The flag can be configured via environment variable or Helm values, allowing operators to enable or disable the middleware without code changes. This provides flexibility for deployments that may need to control how transfer encoding is handled.
userImpact: Users can now enable or disable transfer encoding middleware via configuration, providing control over request/response handling behavior in different deployment scenarios.
author: ntatsumi

title: Fix mypy violations
summary: Fixed mypy type-checking violations in the codebase to improve type safety and code quality. Changes include updates to pyproject.toml configuration and minor corrections in server.py, along with dependency lock file updates.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Automate release PR creation and main→dev sync (#release-process)
summary: Added automated release PR creation workflow and main→dev sync step to streamline the release process. The create-release-pr.yml workflow triggers on demand to create a dev→main PR with version bump commits, while the sync step in version-workflow.yml merges origin/main into dev after production squash merges to prevent divergence between branches.
userImpact: Release managers can now trigger release PRs automatically via workflow_dispatch, with automatic protection against duplicate and same-version PRs, and the development branch stays synchronized with main after production releases.

title: Make GENAI_ENGINE_MAX_PAGE_SIZE envar configurable
summary: The GENAI_ENGINE_MAX_PAGE_SIZE environment variable is now fully configurable across all deployment methods (CloudFormation, Helm, and direct configuration). This change adds configuration support in the ML engine settings, updates both ECS and Kubernetes manifests, and includes comprehensive test coverage to validate the new configurability.
userImpact: Users can now customize the maximum page size for the GenAI engine by setting the GENAI_ENGINE_MAX_PAGE_SIZE environment variable during deployment, enabling better control over memory and performance tuning.

title: [no-ref] update traces table to allow selecting rows to test with an eval
summary: Enhanced the traces table to support row selection, enabling users to pick specific trace records and initiate eval test runs directly from the traces view. Added a new EvalPickerDialog component and SelectionActionBar to provide an intuitive interface for selecting multiple traces and starting evaluations with those selected traces as inputs.
userImpact: Users can now select rows in the traces table and start eval runs using only the selected traces, streamlining the workflow for testing specific trace records.
author: mcgrawia

title: [no-ref] fix eval pagination
summary: Fixed a pagination bug in continuous evaluations where repository methods were returning only paginated result counts instead of total matching record counts. The frontend's pagination table was using the page size as the total, causing it to always display only one page. Both the repository layer and route handlers have been updated to correctly compute and return the total count before pagination is applied.
userImpact: Users can now navigate through multiple pages of continuous evaluation results correctly, with the frontend accurately reflecting the total number of available records.
author: mcgrawia

title: [no-ref] support user_id filtering by substring instead of exact match
summary: Added support for filtering trace user IDs by substring matching instead of requiring exact matches. This change improves query flexibility when searching traces. Additionally, fixed a model loading issue that prevented the service from running locally on M5 Macs when using the MPS device for SentenceTransformer inference.
userImpact: Users can now filter traces by partial user ID matches, and the service will run correctly on M5 Macs without MPS device errors.
author: mcgrawia

title: fix(ui): add infinite scroll to All Tasks page
summary: The All Tasks page was truncating results at 50 tasks due to a hard-coded page size with no pagination mechanism. This update implements IntersectionObserver-based infinite scroll that automatically fetches the next batch of 50 tasks when the user scrolls near the bottom of the grid. The archived tasks dialog was also bumped to page_size=500 to prevent similar truncation.
userImpact: Users can now view all tasks on the All Tasks page without hitting a 50-task limit, with new tasks loading automatically as they scroll.
author: hashnadz

title: Track violation count per alert rule in compliance metrics
summary: The `policy_alert_rule_check_count` compliance metric now accurately tracks the actual number of violations per alert rule instead of always reporting 1.0. Updated the alert rule checking logic to count all violations within a 24-hour window per rule and enhanced logging with model ID, assignment ID, and rule details for better observability.
userImpact: Users can now see the true violation count per alert rule in compliance metrics, enabling better understanding of which rules are generating the most violations.
author: videetparekh

title: feat: add configurable interval to policy alert rules
summary: Added configurable interval field to policy alert rules, allowing users to specify how frequently alerts are checked instead of using hardcoded defaults. The API now accepts `interval` on create/update, and the database stores `interval_unit` and `interval_count` with sensible defaults (1 day). Materialization and bulk-update logic now respect the stored interval values.
userImpact: Users can now customize the check interval for policy alert rules when creating or updating them, with the system defaulting to 1 day for backward compatibility.
author: videetparekh1995

title: add engine audit logs to sts dev deployment
summary: Engine audit logging is now enabled for the STS Arthur dev deployment. This change allows the team to test audit logging functionality for the genai-engine in a development environment before rolling out to production.
userImpact: Engine audit logs are now captured in the STS dev deployment, enabling validation of audit logging behavior before production deployment.
author: talerez

title: chore: adjust aurora cpu alarm configuration
summary: Aurora RDS CPU alarm configuration has been adjusted to support configurable datapoints with new defaults. This provides more flexibility in alarm sensitivity and evaluation behavior without changing the immediate alert behavior.
userImpact: Users can now configure the number of datapoints evaluated for Aurora CPU alarms, enabling fine-tuned alert thresholds based on their monitoring requirements.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Add --frozen flag on uv sync calls to install exactly what's locked in the lockfile
summary: Added --frozen flag to all uv sync calls across Dockerfiles and CI/CD pipelines to ensure exact reproducibility by installing dependencies exactly as specified in the lockfile. This prevents unintended version updates and improves build determinism.
userImpact: Builds and Docker images now install exact locked dependency versions, eliminating surprises from minor version updates and ensuring reproducible deployments.
author: nori2

title: AuthZ explainer doc
summary: Added comprehensive authorization (AuthZ) explainer documentation to help users understand the authorization system and its capabilities. This new guide provides clear, product-focused documentation for the authorization module.

All updates are patch or minor version changes to maintain stability while bringing in bug fixes and new functionality.
userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes and minor enhancements in key dependencies, with improved API compatibility and reliability across authentication, data validation, and AWS SDK layers.

title: feat: add init container to set volume permissions
summary: Added an init container to the Arthur Scope application on ECS to set proper permissions on the audit log volume/file. This ensures the audit log volume is correctly configured before the main application starts, preventing permission-related failures.
userImpact: Audit logging now works reliably without permission-related errors when the application initializes.

summary: Updated the Upsolve dashboard patch file to include new dashboards. The patch file was replaced with a newer version (upsolve-patch-2026041615281.ucf) that contains the latest dashboard configurations.

title: feat: parameterize hardcoded ports in docker-compose services
summary: Parameterized hardcoded port numbers and bootstrap credentials in docker-compose services using environment variable substitution with sensible defaults (KEYCLOAK_PORT, VITE_PORT, GENAI_ENGINE_PORT, GENAI_UI_PORT, and authentication settings). This enables arthur-stack's multi-stack port offset system to work with docker-compose deployments while maintaining backward compatibility through defaults that preserve existing standalone behavior.
userImpact: Users can now configure service ports and bootstrap credentials via environment variables when running docker-compose, enabling flexible multi-stack deployments with port offsets without breaking existing setups.

title: feat: add filters to list policies endpoint (UP-4244)
summary: Added three new query parameters to the list policies endpoint: `owner_group_id`, `compliance_status`, and `model_id`. The `compliance_status` and `model_id` filters join through policy-model assignments with DISTINCT to avoid duplicates, while `owner_group_id` performs a simple equality filter on the policies table. The existing case-insensitive name search remains unchanged.
userImpact: Users can now filter policies by owner group, compliance status, and model ID when listing policies, enabling more targeted policy discovery and management.

title: [no-ref] add policy resource to upsolve token API
summary: Added "policy" as a supported resource type for the Upsolve token API to enable the policy overview dashboard. The token now returns all model IDs that a user can access with a given policy assigned.

title: add a new log group for audit logs in cloudwatch
summary: Adds a new CloudWatch log group for the audit log system within the scope deployment. The log group is configured with a Retain deletion policy to preserve audit logs for 365 days even after stack teardown, ensuring compliance and auditability.

title: feat: split compliance rule ID into policy rule ID and materialized rule ID
summary: ComplianceRuleSummary now separates the policy rule ID from the materialized alert rule ID. The `id` field returns the policy rule definition ID, while a new `materialized_rule_id` field returns the actual alert rule ID on the model (fetchable via the REST API), resolved via a join on `DBComplianceRuleHistory`. This aligns compliance responses with production data plane behavior.
userImpact: API clients can now distinguish between policy rule definitions and their materialized instances, and fetch detailed alert rule information using the materialized_rule_id.
author: videetparekh1995

title: feat: policy compliance history endpoint
summary: Adds a new `GET /v1/policies/{policy_id}/compliance_history` endpoint that returns the full rule compliance evaluation history as an infinite resource list. Includes a reusable `list_compliance_history` repository method with list-based filters, new `POLICY_LIST_COMPLIANCE_HISTORY` permission in SpiceDB schema and roles, and `created_at` field on compliance records for history timestamp visibility.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve the complete compliance evaluation history for a policy with filtering capabilities, and SpiceDB connectivity is now configurable via environment variables for better deployment flexibility.
author: videetparekh1995

title: [no-ref] fix datasketches compilation on 2.26.1
summary: Fixed datasketches compilation errors that occur when building with TimescaleDB 2.26.1. The Dockerfile was updated to resolve incompatibilities introduced in the 2.26.1 release.
userImpact: Users can now successfully build and deploy the metrics database with TimescaleDB 2.26.1 without encountering datasketches compilation failures.

title: feat: add workspace compliance overview endpoint (UP-4155)
summary: Adds a new `GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/compliance` endpoint that provides a comprehensive, flat compliance table with per-rule and per-model status across all policies in a workspace. The implementation includes a normalized `compliance_rule_statuses` table (with Alembic migration and backfill), dual-write synchronization to keep the JSON column and table in sync, and support for filtering (status, policy, model, rule_type, rule_id, frequency, text search), sorting, pagination, and summary rollup counts. A new `WORKSPACE_LIST_POLICY_COMPLIANCE` permission and SpiceDB schema updates enable proper authorization.
userImpact: Users can now view a unified compliance overview across all rules and models in a workspace with advanced filtering, sorting, and pagination capabilities, enabling better visibility into policy compliance status and trends.

title: create audit logging middleware
summary: Audit logging middleware now captures HTTP request and response data, including the request path, authenticated user, URL path parameters, and response resource IDs. This enables comprehensive audit trails for compliance and debugging purposes, with configuration options to enable/disable logging and expose settings via environment variables.
userImpact: Users can now track which API endpoints were called, by whom, with what resource identifiers, and what was returned in responses for audit and compliance purposes.

summary: Enforces a 3-day package minimum requirement for uv dependency resolution across the project. This constraint is added to both scope/app_plane and scope/arthur-client/python project configurations, ensuring more stable and tested package versions are resolved during dependency installation.
userImpact: Users will now benefit from more stable and mature package versions when using uv for dependency resolution, as only packages with at least 3 days of availability are considered.

Host-mapped ports in docker-compose.yaml are now parameterized for postgres, timescale, and app-plane services.

title: Fix alert_interval migration ORM breakage
summary: The `alert_interval` migration was using SQLAlchemy ORM models to query and update rows, causing fresh database bootstraps to fail when new columns (like `policy_alert_rule_id`) were added to `DBAlertRule` after the migration was written. The migration now uses raw SQL UPDATE statements that are decoupled from the evolving ORM model, allowing the migration chain to complete successfully.
userImpact: Fresh database bootstraps now complete successfully without UndefinedColumn errors, while existing deployments with the migration already applied remain unaffected.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Bump upsolve to 0.4.24
summary: Upsolve image has been upgraded from 0.4.23 to 0.4.24, with the database patch file updated to match the new version. This ensures compatibility between the container image and database schema changes.
userImpact: Upsolve services now run with the latest 0.4.24 image, including any performance improvements or bug fixes in this release.
author: videetparekh1995

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major versions of boto3 (1.42.74 → 1.42.78), numpy (2.4.3 → 2.4.4), and types-requests (2.32.4.20260324 → 2.33.0.20260327). These are routine dependency updates managed by Renovate, including minor AWS API changes and type stub improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates maintain compatibility and include latest AWS API definitions and type hints.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Add scheduled compliance job bootstrapping
summary: Adds a new `SCHEDULE_COMPLIANCE_JOBS` job kind that automatically bootstraps a per-model compliance schedule chain when a policy assignment is created. The schedule uses deterministic UUID5 generation keyed to the model, ensuring idempotency and preventing duplicate schedules. The feature includes job registration (infinite retries, 45MB, MEDIUM priority), database schema updates, API specs, and a safety mechanism to cancel schedules when all assignments are removed from a model.
userImpact: Compliance jobs are now automatically scheduled and managed at the model level when policy assignments are created, eliminating manual scheduling and reducing race conditions through deterministic schedule IDs.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Clean up the data_plane code that's been migrated to Github
summary: Removed data_plane code that has been migrated to a separate Github repository. This cleanup removes approximately 100 files worth of deprecated code including connectors, job executors, config, and utility modules that are no longer maintained in this codebase.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is a code cleanup of already-migrated functionality.
author: nori2

title: UP-4128 return entire compliance job instead of just ID
summary: The policy_assignment resource now returns the entire compliance job object instead of just its ID. This provides users with immediate access to detailed job information including state, timestamps, trigger details, and related metadata without requiring an additional API call.
userImpact: Users can now view full compliance job details directly in policy assignment responses, eliminating the need for separate API calls to fetch job information.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Submit compliance jobs when policy assignments are created
summary: Automatically submits compliance check jobs when policy assignments are created, and whenever policy alert/attestation rules are created, updated, or deleted. Compliance logic is now centralized in operators rather than routers, ensuring jobs are triggered regardless of the API call site. The `raise_on_empty` parameter allows rule operations on policies without assignments to be no-ops.
userImpact: Users can now create policy assignments and automatically receive a `compliance_job_id` in the response, with compliance checks running immediately; compliance checks are also automatically triggered whenever policy rules are modified or attestations are submitted.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4154 Fire webhook on compliance status transition to NON_COMPLIANT
summary: Webhooks are now fired when a policy assignment transitions to NON_COMPLIANT status and the enforcement delay has expired. The system tracks when notifications are sent via a new `last_violation_notified_at` field on assignments, which is cleared when transitioning back to COMPLIANT. A new `execute_webhook_with_context` method on WebhookExecutor enables compliance-specific template rendering for these notifications.
userImpact: Users can now configure webhooks to be notified when policy assignments become non-compliant, with built-in enforcement delay support and deduplication to avoid duplicate notifications.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 compliance check endpoints
summary: Adds three new compliance check endpoints that allow users to trigger compliance verification jobs for policies, models, and policy assignments. These endpoints return the list of created/queued jobs and track compliance job status on assignment resources, enabling integration of compliance checks into policy management workflows.
userImpact: Users can now trigger compliance checks via POST requests to `/api/v1/policies/:policy_id/check_compliance`, `/api/v1/models/:model_id/check_compliance`, and `/api/v1/policy_assignments/:assignment_id/check_compliance` endpoints.
author: ianmcgraw

summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.42.77 → 1.42.78) and pydantic_core (2.42.0 → 2.44.0). These are routine patch and minor version updates to maintain current library versions with bug fixes and API improvements from upstream packages.

Added created_at filters to model list alerts APIs, restructured the compliance_status object to provide detailed breakdowns of compliant vs non-compliant alert and attestation rules, and fixed several bugs including crashing endpoints and permission issues with the data plane role.
userImpact: Users can now filter alerts by creation date and receive more detailed compliance status information showing which specific alert and attestation rules are compliant or non-compliant, with relevant alert details for non-compliant rules.

userImpact: No direct user impact
