title: Policy assignment endpoints with materialization and authz
summary: Implements policy assignment, attestation, and compliance status API endpoints with full SpiceDB authorization, input validation, and cascade deletion. Includes model existence validation, duplicate assignment detection, required attestation notes, and full user object returns in responses. Splits monolithic operators/repositories into per-resource pairs with cross-resource logic in operators and single-resource DB operations in repositories.
userImpact: Users can now create, list, and delete policy assignments across models with proper authorization checks, create and query attestation records with full user details, and set compliance status with detailed alert/attestation rule results.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add NEEDS_ATTENTION compliance status for enforcement delay period
summary: Introduces `NEEDS_ATTENTION` as a new compliance status for policy assignments with violations during the enforcement delay grace period. This status is informational only—no webhooks or tags are triggered—allowing model owners time to remediate. Once the enforcement delay expires with violations still present, the daily compliance job automatically promotes to `NON_COMPLIANT`.
userImpact: Model owners can now see which policy assignments need remediation during the enforcement delay period before being marked as non-compliant, with clear visibility into the compliance status lifecycle.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-4128 api tweaks for job
summary: Extended the job compliance API with new filtering capabilities for policy assignments, alert rules, and attestations. Added `assignment_id` filters to model list policy assignments and alert rules APIs, included `policy_assignment_id` and `policy_alert_rule_id` fields in alert rules responses, and added `latest=true` and `valid=true` filters to the attestations API to support policy compliance checking workflows.
userImpact: Users can now filter alert rules and attestations by policy assignment and filter attestations by validity status, enabling more efficient compliance job execution and data retrieval.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-4128 add compliance check job API spec
summary: Added API specification support for a new compliance check job type. This includes database schema updates to track policy assignment IDs and updates to the job API models, storage layer, and repository to handle the new job specification.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage compliance check jobs through the API using the new job specification type.
author: ianmcgraw

title: uv for dependency management
summary: Replaced requirements.txt and pip-based dependency management with uv across the app-plane and arthur-client projects. This change enables better dependency pinning, consistency across development and CI environments, and streamlines the development workflow by eliminating manual Python environment setup.
userImpact: Developers can now use uv for faster, more reliable dependency management with locked versions and simplified local development setup.
author: nori2

title: Implement policy alert and attestation rule CRUD with authz and propagation
summary: Implements complete CRUD operations for policy alert rules and attestation rules with full SpiceDB authorization enforcement. Alert rules include static SQL validation and live propagation to materialized `DBAlertRule` rows across all currently assigned models. Attestation rules support creation, listing, updates, and deletion with validation to prevent removal of the last rule on an applied policy.
userImpact: Users can now create, list, retrieve, update, and delete policy alert and attestation rules via dedicated REST endpoints, with authorization checks and automatic propagation of changes to assigned models.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Policy CRUD (Create/Read/Update/Delete) with Inline Rules
summary: Implemented complete Policy CRUD (Create/Read/Update/Delete) functionality with inline rules management. Added `PoliciesRepository` for atomic operations on policies and their alert/attestation rules, `PoliciesOperator` for transaction handling, and wired 5 router endpoints with SpiceDB permission checks. Included comprehensive database translators and endpoint lifecycle tests covering full CRUD, validation, filtering, sorting, and error cases.
userImpact: Users can now create, retrieve, update, and delete policies with inline alert and attestation rules through fully permission-checked REST endpoints, with efficient queries and proper authorization enforcement.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Add an alert rule validation endpoint without modelId requirement
summary: A new alert rule validation endpoint has been added that validates alert rules without requiring a modelId parameter. This enables clients to validate alert rule configurations independently of a specific model context. The endpoint is properly whitelisted from authorization checks while maintaining authentication requirements.
userImpact: Users can now validate alert rule configurations using a dedicated endpoint that doesn't depend on an existing model, allowing for earlier validation in workflows.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Add policy ORM models, database relationships, and migration
summary: Added comprehensive policy management infrastructure including five new ORM models (`DBPolicy`, `DBPolicyAlertRule`, `DBPolicyAttestationRule`, `DBPolicyModelAssignment`, `DBAttestationRecord`), two nullable foreign keys to `DBAlertRule` for policy materialization linkage, and two new authorization relationships (`POLICY_PARENT`, `MODEL_PARENT`) for dual-parent SpiceDB support. Includes a complete Alembic migration creating all tables, columns, indexes, and named constraints for full rollback support.
userImpact: Users can now create organization-scoped governance policies with attached alert and attestation rules, assign policies to models with compliance tracking, and record immutable attestation actions with dual SpiceDB parent relationships.
author: videetparekh1995

title: Use oidc
summary: Replaced access key-based authentication with OpenID Connect (OIDC) across all deployment environments. This change unifies authentication mechanisms and removes dependencies on axios-based STS credentials, improving security and consistency across AWS and other platform deployments.
userImpact: Users and deployment pipelines now authenticate using OIDC instead of static access keys, enabling more secure and streamlined credential management across all environments.
author: jdb-arthur

title: [no-ref] set renovate minimumReleaseAge 3 days
summary: Updated Renovate configuration to set minimumReleaseAge to 3 days before automatically upgrading packages. This provides a grace period for package maintainers to identify and revoke malicious versions before they are adopted by this project.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Atomic policy creation with inline rules and field renames
summary: The policy creation endpoint now accepts alert rules and attestation rules inline at creation time, with at least one rule required, simplifying the workflow to a single atomic operation. Three field renames were applied across design docs and code: `grace_period_days` → `enforcement_delay_days`, `grace_period_ends_at` → `enforcement_starts_at`, and `interval_days` → `validity_period_days` on attestation rules. The create response was simplified to return only `PolicySummary` (id, name, description) instead of the full `Policy` object.
userImpact: Users can now create policies with rules in a single request without requiring a separate assignment step, and policy field names better reflect their semantic meaning.
author: videetparekh1995

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project, including anyio (4.12.1 → 4.13.0), authzed/spicedb (v1.50.0 → v1.51.0), boto3 (1.42.74 → 1.42.75), cfn-lint (1.47.0 → 1.47.1), and timescale/timescaledb (2.25.2 → 2.26.0). Changes were applied to requirements files and container image pins.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Redistribute policy permissions across existing roles
summary: Removed the premature Policy Manager role and redistributed model-level policy permissions (assign, remove, attest, check compliance) to Project Admin only. Organization-level roles (Org Admin, Org Reader) no longer grant any model-level access. Added a new `is_assigned_in` reverse relation enabling model owners to read policies assigned to their models without requiring org-level `policy_read` permission, while preserving cross-model isolation. Added `NEEDS_ATTENTION` compliance status for grace period violations. Removed dead SpiceDB code that was never used in permission resolution.
userImpact: Model-level policy operations (assign, remove, attest, check compliance) now require Project Admin role binding rather than Org Admin; model owners can read assigned policies without org-level permissions; Project Reader role is now read-only (no check_compliance).
author: videetparekh1995

The migration must run before the updated ML engine is deployed to prevent DuckDB BinderException errors when Shield aggregations attempt to SELECT model_name from tables lacking the column in their stored schema.

userImpact: The arthur engine is now automatically deployed to the AWS dev environment with dynamic version management and proper CloudFormation stack orchestration.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Avoid cert copy that's not working in JPMC
summary: Fixed a non-functional certificate copy operation in the JPMC deployment environment. The Dockerfile and docker-entrypoint.sh were updated to remove or skip the problematic cert copy step that was failing in the JPMC context.
userImpact: JPMC deployments no longer fail due to certificate copy errors during container initialization.
author: nori2

title: Postgres HA with streaming replication and auto-failover with Patroni and etcd
summary: Added Docker Compose deployment for a 3-node PostgreSQL high-availability cluster using Patroni and etcd for consensus-based failover. The stack includes streaming replication from primary to replicas and automatic primary failover capabilities, enabling production-ready database redundancy.
userImpact: Users can now deploy a highly available PostgreSQL cluster with automatic failover using the provided RHEL-compatible Docker Compose configuration and Patroni orchestration.
author: nori2

title: add validations and unit tests for custom aggregations
summary: Centralized validations for custom aggregations into a single comprehensive validation function (`validate_custom_aggregation_spec_schema`) that returns all validation passes and failures at once. Removed model-level validations in favor of schema-level validation, improving user experience by showing all issues simultaneously rather than one at a time. Added extensive unit test coverage for custom aggregation validation scenarios.
userImpact: Users configuring custom aggregation schemas now receive all validation errors at once when calling the custom aggregations endpoint, eliminating the tedious cycle of fixing one error and resubmitting.
author: talerez

title: Ssl bypass client
summary: Added support for SSL verification bypass in OAuth authentication by introducing a `verify_ssl` parameter across DeviceAuthorizer, OAuthApiConfig, and session configuration. This allows users to configure SSL certificate verification behavior when establishing OAuth sessions, which is useful in development or restricted network environments.
userImpact: Users can now disable SSL certificate verification in OAuth sessions by setting the verify_ssl parameter to False when needed.
author: ibrahim109

title: Refactor SSL context configuration in JWKClient to ensure verify_mode is set...
summary: Fixed SSL context configuration in JWKClient to correctly set verify_mode when SSL verification is disabled. This ensures that the SSL verification setting is properly applied during JWT key retrieval, preventing potential misconfigurations.
userImpact: SSL verification settings in JWKClient are now applied correctly, preventing authentication failures when SSL verification is intentionally disabled.
author: ibrahim109

title: Implement SSL context configuration for JWKClient based on Keycloak SSL...
summary: Refactored SSL context handling in JWKClient to support separate configuration for verified and non-verified SSL connections. This improves flexibility when integrating with Keycloak instances that may have different SSL validation requirements.
userImpact: Users can now configure SSL context behavior in JWKClient to handle both verified and unverified SSL connections appropriately based on their Keycloak setup.
author: ibrahim109

title: Implement SSL context configuration for JWKClient based on Keycloak SSL...
summary: Added SSL context configuration support to JWKClient to respect Keycloak SSL verification settings via environment variable. The JWKClient now reads the `KEYCLOAK_SSL_VERIFY` environment variable to control whether SSL certificates are validated when communicating with Keycloak, with a default value of `true` in settings.yaml for secure-by-default behavior.
userImpact: Users can now control SSL certificate verification for Keycloak integration by setting the KEYCLOAK_SSL_VERIFY environment variable, enabling secure or flexible deployment scenarios.
author: ibrahim109

title: UP-2994: Support for nested metric arguments
summary: Added support for nested metric arguments in the metric configuration system. This enhancement allows metrics to accept complex nested argument structures, enabling more sophisticated metric definitions and integrations with partner systems.
userImpact: Users can now define and use metrics with nested argument structures, providing greater flexibility in metric configuration and integration capabilities.
author: alex380125

title: Retrieve Keycloak SSL verification setting from environment variable
summary: The Keycloak client initialization now retrieves SSL verification settings from an environment variable instead of using a hardcoded value. This allows operators to configure SSL certificate verification behavior without code changes, improving deployment flexibility and security configuration management.
userImpact: Users can now control Keycloak SSL certificate verification through environment variables during deployment, enabling secure configuration across different environments (development, staging, production) without code modifications.
author: ibrahim109

title: fix: remove entrypoint override

title: Eliminate the use of cp command. It's not working in the JPMC environment and we can't reproduce it.

title: Fix alarms

title: add aggregation timeout config to scope
summary: Adds support for configuring the timeout for custom and default aggregations in arthur-scope. The timeout configuration is exposed through environment variables and CloudFormation templates, enabling operators to control aggregation behavior without requiring code changes.
userImpact: Users can now configure aggregation timeout values for both custom and default aggregations via environment configuration in arthur-scope deployments.
author: talerez

title: UP-815 idp group management implementation
summary: Implements IDP-managed group membership, allowing groups to optionally be managed by identity provider claims in user tokens instead of directly in Arthur. When a group has an `idp_group_mapping_name` set, membership is controlled via the `arthur_idp_groups` token claim passed to SpiceDB using caveated relationships. Includes SpiceDB schema migration, token parsing updates, Keycloak bootstrap configuration for new environments, and comprehensive tests.
userImpact: Users can now configure groups to be managed by their identity provider, enabling automatic group membership updates based on user claims in authentication tokens instead of manual Arthur administration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Always use Postgres SSL connection for prod grade deployments
summary: Updated CloudFormation and Helm configurations to enforce PostgreSQL SSL connections for production-grade deployments. This ensures secure database communication in production environments by requiring encrypted connections to the database backend.
userImpact: Production deployments now automatically enforce SSL encryption for PostgreSQL connections, improving database security and compliance for production workloads.
author: nori2

title: Postgres ssl cert folder permission fix
summary: Fixed Postgres SSL certificate folder permission issues in the arthur-auth module and Dockerfile configurations. The fix ensures proper permission setup for SSL certificates used in Postgres connections, preventing authentication failures.
userImpact: Users can now successfully authenticate to Postgres when using SSL certificates without encountering permission-related connection errors.
author: nori2

title: UP-815 tech design for IDP managed authz
summary: Added comprehensive technical design documentation for implementing IDP-managed authorization in the platform. The design outlines how group memberships can be defined and synchronized from customer identity providers (such as Keycloak with OIDC), with group permissions configured within Arthur while group membership is managed externally. Includes detailed setup instructions, configuration examples, and architectural considerations for supporting both future implementations and current manual group management workflows.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This documentation establishes the technical foundation for future IDP-managed group access functionality, while the current implementation continues to use manual group management strategies.
author: ianmcgraw

userImpact: Developers can now use the updated Python client library with support for new Chat and UserManagement API endpoints introduced in OpenAPI spec 2.1.59.
author: martin452

title: Dockerhub publish
summary: Re-enabled Docker Hub publishing for the ml-engine service after it was previously disabled. The CI/CD pipeline now publishes docker images with latest-dev tags to Docker Hub, with proper authentication handling and login order adjustments.
userImpact: Users can now pull the latest ml-engine docker images directly from Docker Hub.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2839: Implement PUT method for custom aggregations
summary: Implemented the PUT method for custom aggregations, enabling users to update existing aggregation configurations via API. The implementation adds the necessary endpoint handling, request/response models, and repository layer support with minimal code changes.
userImpact: Users can now update custom aggregations using the PUT HTTP method, completing the full CRUD lifecycle for this resource.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: UP-2844: Validate if model and custom aggregation are in the same workspace
summary: Added workspace validation when adding custom aggregations as metrics to models. The system now ensures that both the model and custom aggregation resources exist in the same workspace before allowing them to be linked together, preventing invalid cross-workspace metric configurations.
userImpact: Users can now safely add custom aggregations as metrics to models with confidence that workspace isolation rules are enforced, preventing configuration errors from mismatched workspace contexts.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: [NO-REF]: Bump arthur-common to 2.1.55
summary: Updated arthur-common dependency to version 2.1.55 across test, app plane, and data plane requirements. This is a dependency version bump with passing test coverage.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: Update Retrieval size constant
summary: Updated the maximum data retrieval limit in Scope to 100MB and made it configurable via environment variable. The max retrieval size is now defined as an environment variable with a default value, and can be dynamically configured without code changes.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve larger datasets up to 100MB, and operators can adjust this limit through environment variable configuration.
author: videetparekh1995

title: UP-2912: Grant custom_aggregation_read to engines
summary: Granted the custom_aggregation_read permission to engines by updating authorization roles and SpiceDB schema. This enables engines to read custom aggregations, with a migration included to apply the permission to existing engines in fresh and existing environments.
userImpact: Engines can now read custom aggregations, supporting improved data access capabilities for engine-driven workflows.
author: alex380125

title: Update openapi and arthur-common for agents
summary: Updated arthur-common to version 2.1.54 and openapi.json to version 2.1.53 to support agents. This includes regenerated Python client files and related schema updates to reflect the new API specification, with adjustments to hallucination rules and test fixtures.
userImpact: Users can now use agent-related features through the updated API client with the latest schema definitions.
author: martin452

title: UP-2807: Upsolve upgrade to 0.3.29 and fresh UCF export
summary: Upgraded Upsolve service to version 0.3.29 and fixed the Docker entry point to work with the latest start command. A fresh UCF (Unify Configuration Framework) export has been integrated into the patch files to ensure database schema consistency.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest Upsolve version improvements and bug fixes, with proper Docker startup behavior restored.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [NO-REF]: Config to run integration tests with ml-engine image instead of data plane image
summary: Updated Docker Compose configuration and documentation to run integration tests against the ml-engine image instead of the data plane image. This change includes a new starter Docker Compose job to set up genai_client build for ml-engine with a cached poetry environment, improving integration test setup and consistency with the ml-engine service.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Integration test infrastructure now uses the ml-engine image for test execution.
author: alex380125

title: CV Experimentation Demo
summary: Added a new Computer Vision LLM demonstration in the demos directory that combines image data with language models. This includes dataset building scripts, prediction pipelines, and configuration files to showcase CV+LLM integration patterns for internal reference and experimentation.
userImpact: No direct user impact.

title: UP-2837: Implement POST and GET methods for custom aggregations
summary: Implements POST and GET endpoints for custom aggregations, enabling users to create and retrieve custom aggregations within their workspaces. Includes database model, repository layer with create/get operations, API endpoints, and comprehensive test coverage for the custom aggregations lifecycle.
userImpact: Users can now create and retrieve custom aggregations within their workspace via POST and GET API endpoints.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies to their latest patch and minor versions, including Flask, SQLAlchemy, aiohttp, anyio, Faker, aiosignal, and authzed. This ensures the application benefits from bug fixes, performance improvements, and security patches from upstream dependencies while maintaining API compatibility.

title: UP-2877: Create org-level list datasets endpoint
summary: Added a new organization-level endpoint to list datasets across all workspaces within an organization. The implementation includes database query enhancements to filter datasets by workspace ID, updated permissions and role definitions to grant access to the custom aggregation manager role, and comprehensive test coverage for the new functionality.
userImpact: Users with appropriate permissions can now query datasets at the organization level, viewing all datasets across workspaces in a single request.
author: alex380125

title: Make the RDS force_ssl parameter configurable and set it to enabled on the cluster by default
summary: The RDS force_ssl parameter is now configurable via CloudFormation, with SSL enforcement enabled by default on the cluster. This ensures encrypted database connections and allows operators to override the setting if needed.
userImpact: Database connections to RDS are now encrypted by default, improving security, while allowing operators to disable SSL if required for specific use cases.

title: Make the RDS force_ssl parameter configurable and set it to enabled by default
summary: The RDS force_ssl parameter is now configurable and enabled by default, improving security for database connections. This change allows users to control SSL enforcement while providing secure defaults out of the box.
userImpact: RDS instances will now require SSL connections by default, and users can configure this setting as needed for their deployments.
author: nori2

title: UP-2851: Fix tests for default metrics with reported aggregations field
summary: Updated test fixtures and bumped arthur-common dependency to align with required `reported_aggregations` field in AggregationSpecSchema. This ensures test data matches the updated schema requirements for default metrics.

title: Remove unnecessary envars from Upsolve
summary: Removed unnecessary environment variables from Upsolve Docker Compose configurations. This cleanup simplifies the service setup by eliminating unused envars from both the scoped and root-level docker-compose files.
userImpact: No direct user impact

summary: Removed extraneous PostgreSQL configuration parameters from the Arthur Scope Upsolve ECS CloudFormation stack. This cleanup eliminates unnecessary configuration that could have caused confusion or conflicts in deployments.

These updates include patch fixes and minor feature additions to maintain dependency freshness and security.

title: UP-2846: Create custom aggregations manager role
summary: Introduces a new "custom metrics manager" role that enables fine-grained access control for custom aggregations. The role is defined in the authorization system with corresponding permissions, role bindings, and SpiceDB schema updates to support the new capability.
userImpact: Users can now assign the custom metrics manager role to grant specific team members permissions to manage custom aggregations without requiring broader administrative access.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2836: Create API resources and endpoints for custom aggs
summary: Introduces API resources and endpoints for managing custom aggregations, including CRUD operations, validation, and permissions. Adds new API models, router implementations, authorization roles, and comprehensive test coverage to support custom aggregation management in the application.
userImpact: Users can now create, read, update, and delete custom aggregations through new API endpoints, as well as validate custom aggregation syntax at the workspace level.
author: alex380125

title: Simplify the spicedb docker-entrypoint
summary: The SpiceDB docker entrypoint script was simplified by removing unnecessary logic and cleaning up the initialization process. The Upsolve entrypoint was also updated to fix entry point logic and remove a redundant call to the Upsolve entrypoint script. These changes reduce complexity in the Docker startup procedures.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal Docker container initialization is cleaner and more maintainable.
author: nori2

title: Agentic Trace v1
summary: Introduced agentic trace v1 support to enable metrics calculation for agentic models. The implementation adds task type detection to identify agentic models during task creation, modifies the shield connector to retrieve trace data from the SpansApi, and executes agentic-specific metric computation before standard aggregation. This allows Arthur to monitor and analyze trace-level metrics for agentic AI applications.
userImpact: Users can now create and monitor agentic AI model tasks with automated trace-level metrics calculation and analysis.
author: martin452

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.39.9 → 1.39.12), pydantic_core (2.35.2 → 2.36.0), and timescaledb (2.21.0 → 2.21.1). These patch and minor version updates include bug fixes and new API features in boto3 for EC2, Glue, and Bedrock services.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2829: Improve handling of scheduled job race condition
summary: When scheduled jobs with duplicate `nonce` values were submitted due to race conditions, the system would respond with a 500 error. This change adds validation to prevent submission of jobs with existing nonce values and stops job retries in this scenario, providing more graceful handling instead of server errors.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs that encounter nonce conflicts will be rejected gracefully instead of returning 500 errors, preventing unnecessary retries and improving system stability.
author: alex380125

title: Add SSL connection support for Postgres for Upsolve
summary: Added SSL connection support for Postgres in Upsolve, enabling secure database connections. The implementation includes Docker configuration updates and CloudFormation stack modifications to enforce SSL certificates and environment variable setup for encrypted Postgres connections.
userImpact: Upsolve can now establish secure, encrypted connections to Postgres databases using SSL/TLS.
author: nori2

title: Dockerfile fix - use Alpine as the build image for SpiceDB
summary: Updated the SpiceDB Dockerfile to use Alpine as the build image instead of the previous base image. This reduces the build image size and improves build efficiency for the container image pipeline.
userImpact: SpiceDB container builds are now smaller and faster, with improved image efficiency in production deployments.
author: nori2

title: fix: copy script into/from installer stage
summary: The startup script is now properly copied into the installer stage and then propagated to the final stage in the SpiceDB Dockerfile. This fixes a previous issue where the script was not being included in the final container image.
userImpact: The SpiceDB container now includes the startup script in the final image, ensuring proper initialization behavior.
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: use timescaledb for the gcp artifact registry
summary: Corrected the GCP repository name to use timescaledb for the artifact registry, fixing a misconfiguration in the CI/CD pipeline. This ensures the correct artifact repository is referenced during builds and deployments.
userImpact: Builds and deployments to the GCP artifact registry now correctly target the timescaledb repository.
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: make spicedb image version dynamic for dev
summary: Updated the SpiceDB image version configuration to be dynamic in the dev environment instead of hardcoded. This change modifies the deployment configuration and CI/CD pipeline to support flexible versioning, reducing friction when updating the SpiceDB image.
userImpact: Development deployments can now use flexible SpiceDB image versions without requiring manual configuration updates to the codebase.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Spicedb ssl
summary: SpiceDB now uses SSL to connect to Postgres, enhancing security for database communications. Added support for SSL certificates in the SpiceDB configuration and enforced SSL usage in the ECS stack template.
userImpact: Users can now securely connect SpiceDB to Postgres using SSL, protecting database credentials and data in transit.
author: jdb-arthur

title: SpiceDB Postgres SSL connection support
summary: Added SpiceDB Postgres SSL connection support with a custom container image that automatically imports SSL certificates. Includes CI/CD pipeline configuration and Docker Compose example demonstrating how to set up and use SSL-secured Postgres connections with SpiceDB.
userImpact: Users can now configure SpiceDB to connect to Postgres using SSL/TLS encryption, with automatic certificate management via the provided custom container and example Docker Compose setup.
author: nori2

title: Revert "Revert "UP-2598: Change variable name to be unique" and "UP-2598: Add Slack notification""

The changes restore the ability to send Slack notifications from the app-plane service while correcting the variable naming and configuration across all deployment environments.
userImpact: Slack notifications for project and registration events are now functional again with the correct environment variable configuration.

title: UP-2581: Custom Aggregations tech design
summary: Technical design documentation for Custom Aggregations feature has been finalized, covering API endpoints, security considerations, performance protections, and permission models. The design specifies reusable workspace-level aggregations (Option 2) that can be attached to multiple models, with creation restricted to workspace admins and SQL-based execution for safety.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is technical design documentation that guides implementation of the Custom Aggregations feature.

title: Revert "UP-2598: Change variable name to be unique" and "UP-2598: Add Slack notification"

title: chore: add gcp login for data plane
summary: Added GCP Docker authentication to the data plane CI pipeline configuration. The data plane's before script was overriding the default authentication setup, preventing access to GCP-hosted Docker images during CI/CD operations.

title: UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails
summary: Email addresses are now normalized to lowercase across the system to enforce consistency and prevent duplicate accounts created with different casing. A database migration adds constraints to ensure all stored emails are lowercase, and validation logic in the API and internal models enforces this requirement on new user creation and updates.
userImpact: Users can no longer create multiple accounts using the same email with different casing (e.g., Test@example.com vs test@example.com), and all emails are standardized to lowercase for better account management.

title: Adding dev-sts env and a new convenience deploy script for running the deploy outside the CICD
summary: Added a new dev-sts environment configuration and a convenience deploy script that enables developers to run deployments outside the CI/CD pipeline. This improves developer workflow by allowing local or manual deployment execution with a dedicated environment setup.
userImpact: Developers can now deploy to the dev-sts environment and execute deployments manually using the new deploy script without relying solely on CI/CD automation.

title: Fixup paths in openapi-client Dockerfile
summary: Fixed path references in the openapi-client Dockerfile and updated import paths across multiple Python files and connectors. This addresses issues with Docker build paths and ensures consistent module imports throughout the codebase.

title: UP-2598: Change variable name to be unique

title: Push gcp images
summary: Added automation to push Docker images to Google Cloud Platform (GCP) container registry alongside existing Docker Hub pushes. The implementation includes a reusable GCP login job and integration of push commands into the CI/CD pipeline to streamline multi-registry image deployment.
userImpact: Docker images are now automatically pushed to GCP container registry in parallel with existing registry pushes, enabling broader registry availability.

title: Revert "UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails"
summary: Reverted the enforcement of lowercase email constraints that was causing production failures. The original migration has been emptied to avoid re-running in environments where it previously executed, while allowing it to run safely in environments where it has not yet been applied.
userImpact: Email handling is restored to its previous state, resolving production issues caused by the lowercase email enforcement.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails
summary: Fixed a bug where pending role invitations were never granted to users with uppercase letters in their emails. The issue occurred because Keycloak stores all emails as lowercase, but pending invitations didn't enforce lowercase normalization. This inconsistency caused email matching to fail during the accept-invites flow. The fix normalizes all user emails to lowercase across the platform, matching Keycloak's behavior and ensuring consistent email storage.
userImpact: Users with uppercase letters in their email addresses can now successfully accept pending role invitations during sign up.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2752: Aggregation time segmentation performance protections
summary: Adds validation checks during aggregation processing to ensure segmentation columns meet performance requirements: fewer than 3 columns, appropriate data types, and limited unique values. These protections are now enforced at metric calculation job time before aggregations execute, rather than during aggregation execution itself, providing a consistent safety mechanism for all aggregation implementations including future custom metrics.
userImpact: Users will now receive clear errors if aggregation segmentation columns fail to meet performance requirements (more than 3 columns, incorrect data types, or excessive unique values) before metric calculation begins, preventing silent failures and performance degradation.
author: alex380125

title: chore: bump arthur-common to 2.1.49

summary: Renamed "Scope Client" to "Arthur Client" throughout the codebase to improve naming clarity and consistency. This change updates environment variables, imports, and references across the app_plane package and its tests, and consolidates the client implementation to use arthur_client directly.
userImpact: Users and developers will see consistent "Arthur Client" naming in documentation, code, and error messages instead of the previous "Scope Client" terminology.

title: feat: Add ODBC connector support
summary: Introduces comprehensive ODBC connector support to the Arthur Scope platform, enabling connectivity to any ODBC-compatible database (SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, etc.) through a unified interface. The implementation includes API model updates, connector schema definitions, a complete data plane implementation with support for time-based filtering and pagination, and integration test coverage with PostgreSQL.
userImpact: Users can now connect to any ODBC-compatible database and perform data discovery, schema inspection, filtering, and pagination operations through a single unified connector.
author: ibrahim109

title: UP-2733: ignore optional params in aggregation matcher
summary: Modified the aggregation matcher to ignore optional parameters when validating metric configuration against datasets. This allows metrics with optional parameters (such as segmentation) to be configured on datasets without requiring matching columns for those optional fields. The change enables proper support for segmentation in metric configuration.
userImpact: Users can now configure metrics with optional parameters like segmentation on datasets without needing columns that match every optional parameter.

title: unit test fix: don't use external http server for webhooks
summary: Unit tests for webhooks were using an external httpstat.us server that is currently down, causing flaky test failures. This change replaces the external server dependency with a local HTTP server for more reliable test execution and adds arthur-common to test requirements for integration test compatibility.
userImpact: No direct user impact; unit tests are now more reliable and no longer fail due to external service unavailability.

summary: Added Slack webhook integration to send notifications when users register and models are onboarded. The implementation includes a new `slack.py` module that wraps Slack message delivery, with optional configuration via environment variables and graceful degradation if Slack settings are not configured.
userImpact: Users registering or onboarding models will now trigger Slack notifications to configured channels, enabling real-time monitoring of user and model activity.
